success_surface: Combined iterative exploration, adaptive algorithmic scaling, and comprehensive validation enabled accurate subset-sum computation and safe spreadsheet modification.
effective_behaviors: Systematic data extraction with coordinate tracking before processing
Adaptive combination search that scales range when initial attempts fail
Floating-point tolerant comparison for numerical calculations
Multiple validation checkpoints before saving output
Safe file operations creating new files instead of modifying originals
Pre-action verification of required elements exist before modification
Comprehensive logging of all operations for traceability
Post-save verification by reloading output file
Openpyxl styling applied while preserving existing cell formats
evidence: Agent iterated all rows/columns to extract numeric values with coordinates before subset-sum calculation
Initial search range of 2-6 items expanded to 2-10 when no matches found
Used abs(total - target) < tolerance pattern for floating-point comparisons
Verified C2 value, fill colors, and highlighted cells after modifications
Checked os.makedirs with exist_ok=True before saving
Confirmed Sheet3 presence before deletion, verified removal in final sheet list
Final summary logged all operations, affected cells, and output path
Reloaded saved file with load_workbook to confirm structural integrity
Applied PatternFill without overwriting underlying cell styles or formulas

memory_items.title: Exhaustive Data Discovery Before Processing
memory_items.lesson: Extract and inventory all relevant cell values with their coordinates before performing calculations or modifications
memory_items.generalization: For any spreadsheet analysis task, iterate through the target range first to collect data points with addresses, enabling reliable lookups during subsequent operations.

memory_items.title: Adaptive Algorithm Scaling
memory_items.lesson: When combinatorial searches yield no results, progressively expand search parameters before concluding failure
memory_items.generalization: Implement tiered search strategies that increase scope (e.g., item counts, tolerance ranges) upon initial failure, with fallback to best-match heuristics if no exact solution exists.

memory_items.title: Floating-Point Comparison Tolerance
memory_items.lesson: Use tolerance-based equality checks for numerical comparisons involving computed sums
memory_items.generalization: Always account for floating-point precision errors when comparing calculated values to targets using relative or absolute tolerance thresholds.

memory_items.title: Multi-Stage Validation Pipeline
memory_items.lesson: Verify data at multiple points: before writing, immediately after writing, and after file save
memory_items.generalization: Build verification checkpoints throughout the workflow: pre-modification state check, post-modification content check, and post-save file integrity check.

memory_items.title: Conditional Element Existence Checks
memory_items.lesson: Verify sheets, columns, or elements exist before attempting modification operations
memory_items.generalization: Never assume target structures are present; always programmatically verify existence before delete/move/update operations.

edits.content:
## 6. Success Patterns
- **Exhaustive Inventory**: Extract all relevant cell values with coordinates before processing calculations.
- **Adaptive Search Scaling**: Progressively expand search parameters (range size, tolerance) when initial attempts yield no results.
- **Tolerance-Based Numerical Comparison**: Account for floating-point precision using `abs(actual - target) <= threshold` patterns.
- **Multi-Stage Validation**: Verify at three points—before modification, after modification, and after file save via reload.
- **Existence Guardrails**: Programmatically confirm sheets, ranges, or elements exist before delete/move/update operations.
- **Progressive Troubleshooting**: When exact solutions fail, implement fallback strategies (wider tolerances, closest-match heuristics) before reporting failure.
- **Styling Preservation**: Apply openpyxl styles (fills, borders) without overwriting existing cell formats, formulas, or merged regions.
